steps.title: Find the key signal about effect size
steps.content:
The passage states that for every study, traits were observably different between exposed and unexposed groups, and that both differences are substantial.
This signals that anthropogenic noise produces noticeable effects regardless of whether they help or hurt the animal.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C — exposure to anthropogenic noise will likely have noticeable effects both on mammals such as brown rats and on birds such as European robins, but the nature of that effect could be very different for the two species. says exposure will likely have noticeable effects on mammals and birds, but the effect could be very different for the two species, which matches the idea of substantial differences that may be beneficial or detrimental.
The other three either limit the effect to certain groups, claim larger effects for one group, or add unsupported details about specifying detriment.
That confirms C is correct.
